judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly and thoroughly identify the config/fixture mismatch, the SCORE-77 commit, the wrong-code-path spot-check, and the futile model rollback — they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: config/scoring_config.yaml sets expected_label_field: label, but every record in fixtures/eval_set.jsonl now stores its gold label under the key gold_label (not label). ... The fix is a one-line config change: in ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml, change expected_label_field: label to expected_label_field: gold_label

report_2.evidence: config/scoring_config.yaml still says expected_label_field: label, while every record in fixtures/eval_set.jsonl now uses the key "gold_label" ... The file to change is **ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml**: update expected_label_field: label to expected_label_field: gold_label

report_1.evidence: commit 53551e8 — chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77), dated Wed Jun 17 13:00:00 2026 ... pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 through 2026-06-16 and exactly 0.00 starting 2026-06-17, the same date as the commit

report_2.evidence: pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv shows accuracy going from 0.91 on 2026-06-16 straight to 0.00 on 2026-06-17 ... This lines up exactly with commit 53551e8 — "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", dated Wed Jun 17 13:00:00 2026

report_1.evidence: it re-ran individual tickets through pipeline/score_one.py, but that script (read above) never loads the config and never compares its prediction against any gold label at all ... reproduce at the layer that actually shows the bug — here, that means running the actual batch entry point (pipeline/score_batch.py)

report_2.evidence: pipeline/score_one.py (used for the manual spot-check re-runs) only calls predict_category(normalize_simple(text)) — it never reads scoring_config.yaml, never reads a gold label field, and never performs the prediction == gold comparison ... run the actual nightly entry point, python3 -m pipeline.score_batch

report_1.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it targets the wrong mechanism and will not fix anything. There is no model being invoked in this pipeline at all ... "Reverting the model" would touch a field nothing consumes, leave expected_label_field: label unchanged, and the nightly accuracy would remain exactly 0.0

report_2.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it targets the wrong layer of the system and would not fix anything. ... Swapping model_id or "reverting the model" would leave expected_label_field: label unchanged, fixtures/eval_set.jsonl would still use gold_label, and the batch score would still be 0.0
